content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

December 21, 2011


Robert Hartsock
McMurtrey, Hartsock & Worth
2011 22nd St, Suite 100
Bakersfield, CA 93301

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No.: W-11-228

Dear Mr. Noble:

This letter is in response to your request for written advice on Government Code Section 1090 and its application to potential water sales between the Buena Vista Water Storage District and the Semitropic Water Storage District. Under the provisions of Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), copy enclosed, we must decline your request for advice.

Government Code Section 1090 is outside the purview of the Fair Political Practices Commission, and we advise only on laws in the Political Reform Act.¹ (Government Code sections 81000-91014.) You may wish to discuss your question with the Office of the Attorney General.

In addition, as we discussed in our December 14, 2011 telephone call, Directors Ron Torigiani and John Vidovich may also potentially have conflicts of interests under the Act if they have economic interests in business entities in which they have a direct or indirect investment of $2,000 or more. (Section 87103(a); Regulation 18703.1(a).) Whether the financial effect of a governmental decision on an indirectly involved business entity is material depends on the size of the business entity. The standards vary according to whether the business entity is a Fortune 500 company, is listed on the New York Stock Exchange, American Stock Exchange, or NASDAQ, or according to the size of the business entity. (Regulation 18705.1) If certain financial thresholds are met under

___________________________
¹ For your information we enclose the pamphlet "Can I Vote" which discusses conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act.

Regulation 18705.1, the directors would have a conflict of interest in a given decision and must recuse themselves from participating in the decision.²

If you determine in the future you need conflict of interest advice under the Act, feel free to submit a new advice request. If you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660 .

Sincerely,

Emelyn Rodriguez
Commission Counsel
Legal Division

ER:jgl

Enclosures

___________________________

² If a public official's office is listed in section 87200 and he or she has a conflict of interest in a decision noticed at a public meeting, then he or she must: (1) immediately prior to the discussion of the item, verbally identify each type of economic interest involved in the decision as well as details of the economic interest, as discussed in regulation 18702.5(b)(1)(B), on the record of the meeting; (2) recuse himself or herself; and (3) leave the room for the duration of the discussion and/or vote on the item. For closed sessions, consent calendars, absences and speaking as a member of the public regarding personal interests, special rules found in regulation 18702.5, sub-divisions (c) and (d) apply. (Section 87105.)
Please note that these provisions would apply to water board members only if they manage public investments.

sections.conclusion: Government Code Section 1090 is outside the purview of the Fair Political Practices Commission, and we advise only on laws in the Political Reform Act (Government Code sections 81000-91014). The requester is advised to contact the Office of the Attorney General regarding Section 1090. Regarding potential conflicts of interest under the Act for Directors Torigiani and Vidovich: if they have direct or indirect investments of $2,000 or more in business entities affected by a governmental decision, and the financial effect meets materiality thresholds under Regulation 18705.1, they would have a conflict of interest and must recuse themselves from participating in the decision.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked for FPPC advice on Government Code Section 1090 and its application to potential water sales between two water storage districts, and raised concerns about potential conflicts of interest for two directors with economic interests in related business entities.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to advise on Government Code Section 1090 as it falls outside the Political Reform Act. However, the FPPC provides guidance that the two named directors may have conflicts of interest under the Act if they have $2,000+ investments in affected business entities and the financial impact meets materiality standards under Regulation 18705.1, in which case they must recuse themselves.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections, but contains a clear legal question and FPPC response integrated into the letter body. The file number format (W-11-228) and reference to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D) confirm this is a formal declination. Addressee discrepancy noted: letter addressed to 'Mr. Noble' but on letterhead for 'Robert Hartsock' of McMurtrey, Hartsock & Worth firm.

QUESTION: The requester asked for FPPC advice on Government Code Section 1090 and its application to potential water sales between two water storage districts, and raised concerns about potential conflicts of interest for two directors with economic interests in related business entities.

CONCLUSION: Government Code Section 1090 is outside the purview of the Fair Political Practices Commission, and we advise only on laws in the Political Reform Act (Government Code sections 81000-91014). The requester is advised to contact the Office of the Attorney General regarding Section 1090. Regarding potential conflicts of interest under the Act for Directors Torigiani and Vidovich: if they have direct or indirect investments of $2,000 or more in business entities affected by a governmental decision, and the financial effect meets materiality thresholds under Regulation 18705.1, they would have a conflict of interest and must recuse themselves from participating in the decision.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines a request for advice on Government Code Section 1090 regarding water sales between two districts, as that statute is outside the FPPC's jurisdiction. The letter provides guidance on potential conflicts of interest for two directors under the Political Reform Act based on their business interests.
